Shot 2 captures the instant of impact—the punch lands against the glass and water ripples outward. Shot 3 reveals the aftermath, micro-fractures forming in the pane and tiny bubbles scattering from the impact. Together, the sequence demonstrates the mantis shrimp's incredible punching power and the fragility of the glass barrier, anchoring the topic in a single, continuous subject.

feedback:
The sequence stays on the mantis shrimp and the glass tank, and the narration adds context about the impact, but the timestamp structure and shot-count diverge from the gold standard (longer, non-standard intervals and an extra shot). To align, compress into clearer, standard 4-second segments and use two concise shots: setup close-up, then ultra-fast strike with cavitation visible on the glass. Also tighten camera angles to include a macro or tracking shot option for added cinematic variety and ensure the wording mirrors the concise, action-first style of the gold reference.
